label: Wide-shell 3BR / baths DISTRIBUTED with east-west CENTER SPINE hall — the classic PH wide bungalow parti: two room bands separated by a spine corridor. Great_room (entry) + master across the front, kitchen + common T&B + standard pair across the rear. Open-plan great_room + kitchen (gr style).

adjacencies.note: ensuite is an alcove at master's front-east corner (east gable wall for venting) — interior door from master
master's rear wall doors north into the spine — master is the only bedroom in the front band
br2 doors south into the spine
br3 doors south into the spine at its east end
standard pair sits side-by-side in the rear band — shared wall only, no door
common T&B doors south into the spine — DEFAULT host of the 'common_access' group
common shares the wet wall with kitchen (stack 1). ALTERNATE door host: only when shared wall >= 1.5 m; falls back to the spine on compact shells
the spine's west end mouths into the great_room (codebase rule: hallway↔great_room is always open)
great_room (front-west) and kitchen (rear-west) connect open-plan west of the spine's start (gr style)

soft_proximities.note: keep the shared bath next to the standard pair
nudge the two east-side wet points toward each other to shorten the under-slab run on the east half

lot_adjustment_profiles.name: compact_wd_center_spine_baths_ds_hall_gr
lot_adjustment_profiles.rationale: The spine is the fixed cost of this parti (~1.0 x 6-7 m of pure circulation), so the profile triggers early (105 m²). Spine depth floors at 1.0 m; kitchen galley at 1.8 m; master capped at 15 m² so the rear pair holds >= 2.9 m widths. Below ~88 m² the spine cannot pay for itself — fall back to half_spine.

notes: 3BR wide catalog topology #1 — CENTER SPINE, the classic PH wide bungalow.
Natural floor band ~88-100 m²; canonical test shell 12.0 x 8.0 m (96 m²) on a
16.5 x 14.5 m lot; minimum feasible ~11.4 x 7.8 m before fallback.
Layout intent (canonical, west = left):
Rear:  [kitchen | common T&B | br2   | br3           ]
Front: [great_room (entry)  | master      : ensuite  ]
Program: two full-width bands. Front band: great_room hosts entry on the
west, master + ensuite alcove on the east (street-facing master — the
traditional poblacion arrangement). Rear band west-to-east: kitchen, common
T&B, then the standard pair. The east-west spine threads between the bands,
starting east of the great_room↔kitchen open-plan connection, so the public
west half stays spine-free.
Why depth 7.5-8.5 m is the sweet band: 3.3 m room + 1.0 m spine + 3.3 m room
+ walls. Deeper drifts squarish; shallower breaks the two-band section.
Ventilation: every habitable room holds either the front or rear wall; the
spine borrows light from the great_room mouth. No landlocked rooms.
Plumbing: distributed — kitchen+common share stack 1 (with the door_host_group
alternate letting common door off the kitchen wall on generous shells);
ensuite on the east gable is stack 2, softly pulled toward br3's wall.
Carport: NCP base form like the 2BR wide series — declare via brief
(side_setback carport on the east, or front-parallel with 3.0 m front
setback). Mirror when brief.carport_side='left'.
